chapter_title.en: Chapter 52 – Inheritance of the son of the cursed mother (by her husband)

text: عِدَّةٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِنَا عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ زِيَادٍ عَنِ ابْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ رِئَابٍ عَنِ الْحَلَبِيِّ قَالَ سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ رَجُلٍ لَاعَنَ امْرَأَتَهُ وَ هِيَ حُبْلَى فَلَمَّا وَضَعَتْ ادَّعَى وَلَدَهَا وَ أَقَرَّ بِهِ وَ زَعَمَ أَنَّهُ مِنْهُ قَالَ يُرَدُّ إِلَيْهِ وَلَدُهُ وَ لَا يَرِثُهُ وَ لَا يُجْلَدُ لِأَنَّ اللِّعَانَ قَدْ مَضَى .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Ali Bin Raib, from Al Halby who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> about a man who cursed his wife and she was pregnant. So when she placed (gave birth), he claimed her child and accepted it and claimed that it is from him’. He<sup>asws</sup>, ‘His child is returned to him, and he (father) would not inherit him (the child), and he would not be whipped because the curse is past’.
